text: Retrospective validation ('Step 12') is structurally broken: no trigger, no session continuity, no ownership, and debate artifacts may be gitignored. Will not happen reliably.
Blind spot accumulation risks becoming taxonomy project rather than behavior change. Accumulation without mandatory consumption in Step 2 is a warehouse of hindsight.
Catch rate denominator is not fixed: if Codex becomes more exhaustive, catch rate falls even if self-review improved. Unsafe for longitudinal comparison.
Specificity score (file:line reference ratio) is gameable and incentivizes over-anchoring on quoted code while underweighting systemic reasoning.
'Validated in implementation' does not cleanly mean false positive. Avoided failures, design changes, and unobserved latent risks all look like non-manifestation.
INDEX variance (0%–57%) suggests domain-sensitivity, not one stable Claude blind spot. A single global intervention is the wrong level.
Stronger Step 2 with a fixed topic-specific adversarial checklist is simpler and more credible than the proposed machinery. Missed entirely in the draft.
Mandatory draft sections (assumptions, failure modes, alternatives rejected, unknowns) improve the substrate before any review runs. Higher leverage than retrospective mechanisms. Missed entirely.
The existing 'impact' field in the critique log (changed/acknowledged/rejected) is a better and more available signal than implementation retrospectives. Missed entirely in the draft.
Periodic human synthesis every 5–10 debates is more sustainable than continuous per-debate blind-spot logging.
Selection discipline (better triage on when to debate) may improve signal quality more than any retrospective mechanism.
Revised retrospective via Munin trigger creates new hard coupling (skill → memory_orient) that doesn't exist in the current skill. This is an architecture change, not a small repair.
The 'impact' field is not schema-clean across existing logs: values include 'deferred', 'partially_changed', 'reframed' beyond the three specified. Needs normalization before use as primary metric.
